# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our Python web application. Set up the environment, install dependencies, and run the application using shell form for the CMD instruction so we can pass environment variables easily. Example CMD python app.py $PORT

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

```Dockerfile
# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:3-slim

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Add source code files into the context of the build 
ADD . /app/src   # assuming your application is at root level with src folder. Modify as per requirement   

# Install production dependencies using requirements file (provided) in a non-root user account, and set working directory to app dir for installs too    
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir -r /app/requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list   
# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./python_modules"] && cd ./python_modules \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list   
# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./node_modules"] && cd ./node_modules \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list   
# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./frontend/node_modules"] && cd ./frontend/node_modules \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list   
# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./backend/python_modules"] && cd ./backend/python_modules \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list   
# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./database"] && cd ./database\n \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list   
# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./other_dependencies"] && cd ./other_dependencies \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list

# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./"] && cd /app/src\n \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list   
# RUN ["pip", "install","--no-cache-dir" , "-t=./"] && cd /app/src\n \   and modify as per requirement    
RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list

RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list

RUN pip3 install --user . -r requirements.txt  # assuming your application has its own 'requirements' list
